title: Intensely Fermented Polish Kippers Bowl
description: A rich, velvety bowl featuring fermented kippers, bonito shavings, and sauerkraut, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This flexitarian dish combines the umami flavors of fermented fish with the crunch of fresh vegetables.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the sauerkraut, caraway seeds, and apple cider vinegar. Let it sit at room temperature for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a small skillet over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and cook until caramelized, stirring occasionally, for about 20 minutes.
In a separate skillet, toast the rye bread crumbs over medium heat until crispy and golden brown, stirring frequently, for about 5 minutes.
To assemble the bowls, place a portion of the fermented kippers on the bottom, followed by a spoonful of the sauerkraut mixture, some caramelized onion, and a sprinkle of toasted rye bread crumbs.
Garnish with chopped fresh dill, parsley, and a sprinkle of black pepper. Serve immediately and enjoy!
Note: The fermented kippers can be prepared ahead of time and stored in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. The sauerkraut mixture can be refrigerated for up to 1 week.
To make the bonito shavings more intense, you can toast them in a dry skillet over medium heat for a few minutes before shaving them over the bowl.
If you prefer a creamier sauce, you can add a tablespoon or two of crème fraîche or sour cream to the sauerkraut mixture before serving.
To make this dish more substantial, you can add some cooked sautéed mushrooms or diced potatoes to the bowl.
For a spicier version, you can add some diced jalapeños or red pepper flakes to the sauerkraut mixture.
To make this dish more indulgent, you can add some crispy bacon or diced ham to the bowl.
To make this dish more substantial, you can add some cooked quinoa or brown rice to the bowl.
